ontology.foodex2_description: Hydrolysed collagen peptides — enzymatically processed bovine or marine collagen, dry powder supplement. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Protein preparations) used as closest defensible parent category for processed animal protein supplement / gelatin derivative.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for hydrolysed collagen peptide supplements. A0CKP (Protein preparations) is the closest parent category available in the FoodEx2 hierarchy. This code is used as a defensible anchor; an exact match does not exist.

preparation_method: enzymatic hydrolysis of collagen-rich animal tissue (bovine hide or fish skin/scales)
no cooking required — dissolves in liquid
main_ingredients: bovine hide collagen (Type I) or marine fish skin/scale collagen (Type I)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10g of hydrolysed collagen peptide powder (approximately 1 sachet or 1 level scoop), typically dissolved in water, juice, or a hot beverage, providing approximately 9g collagen protein and 38 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Gelatin, dry powder, unsweetened (FDC ID 169567)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central gelatin dry powder unsweetened (FDC ID 169567) used as the closest approved-source equivalent for hydrolysed collagen peptides. Hydrolysed collagen peptides are enzymatically processed gelatin derivatives with essentially identical macronutrient profile: approximately 90g protein per 100g, 0g fat, 0g carbohydrate, approximately 380 kcal per 100g. The amino acid profile (glycine ~26g/100g, proline ~15g/100g, hydroxyproline ~14g/100g) is consistent with USDA gelatin data and published collagen biochemistry. Micronutrient values (sodium ~100mg, potassium ~10mg, calcium ~10mg per 100g) are low and consistent with commercial collagen powder specifications. Clinical efficacy context (skin elasticity, joint health) referenced from Choi FY et al. 2019 Nutrients meta-analysis of 19 RCTs — cited for clinical context only, not primary nutrient values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — hydrolysed collagen peptides contain 0g carbohydrate per 100g; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 169567 (gelatin, dry powder, unsweetened)
gi_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
gi_source.source_match_notes: Hydrolysed collagen peptides are a pure protein supplement containing 0g carbohydrate per 100g (confirmed per USDA FoodData Central gelatin FDC 169567). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates — consistent with the Project Veritas critical rule for zero-carbohydrate foods. No GI testing is performed or required for zero-carbohydrate protein supplements. Glycemic index set to null; gi_category set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per 10g serving = 0.0 (confirmed zero carbohydrate content).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~10mg per 10g serving); no clinically significant blood pressure effect at standard doses
hypertension.guidance: Hydrolysed collagen peptides are suitable for individuals with hypertension: sodium content is very low (~100mg per 100g, ~10mg per 10g serving), and while some gelatin-derived bioactive peptides (Gly-Pro-Hyp, Pro-Hyp-Gly) have demonstrated ACE-inhibitory activity in vitro and in animal models, no significant antihypertensive effect has been confirmed in human RCTs at standard supplementation doses of 5–15g per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content; GI not applicable; glycine content may provide modest incretin potentiation via GLP-1 secretagogue mechanism
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hydrolysed collagen peptides are suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: the supplement contains 0g carbohydrate per serving (GI not applicable, no glycaemic impact), and glycine (~3g per 10g serving) acts as a GLP-1 secretagogue via intestinal L-cell glycine receptors, acutely increasing GLP-1 by approximately 20–30% in healthy subjects (Gribble et al. 2016), though this incretin effect is not therapeutically significant compared to standard antidiabetic medications and does not replace pharmacological management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, very low sodium; no adverse cardiovascular nutrient profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hydrolysed collagen peptides present a low cardiovascular risk profile: the supplement contains 0g fat, 0g saturated fat, 0mg cholesterol, and only ~10mg sodium per 10g serving; no adverse lipid or blood pressure effects have been documented in clinical literature at standard doses, and the supplement is appropriate for use in cardiovascular disease management plans as a protein source, provided it is not used as a substitute for complete dietary protein.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-Hyp dipeptides stimulate chondrocyte collagen and hyaluronic acid synthesis; glycine has modest anti-inflammatory macrophage-suppressing properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Collagen peptides may provide secondary benefit in rheumatoid arthritis through Pro-Hyp dipeptide stimulation of chondrocyte hyaluronic acid and collagen synthesis (Ohara 2010) and glycine-mediated suppression of macrophage TNF-α and IL-6 secretion (Zhong 2003), though the primary evidence base for collagen peptides in joint health is for osteoarthritis and sports-related joint pain, not immune-mediated RA synovitis; patients should continue disease-modifying antirheumatic drug (DMARD) therapy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hydrolysed collagen peptides (bovine or marine) are a functional protein supplement produced by enzymatic hydrolysis of collagen-rich animal tissue — bovine hide (Type I collagen, predominantly from South America, Australia, and Europe) or marine fish skin and scales (Type I collagen, predominantly from North Atlantic and Pacific species including tilapia, cod, salmon, and pollock). The hydrolysis process cleaves native collagen triple-helix structures into short-chain peptides with average molecular weight of 2,000–5,000 Da, yielding a water-soluble, tasteless-to-mildly-savoury powder that dissolves readily in hot or cold liquids. Per 100g dry powder: approximately 380 kcal, 90g protein (NOT a complete protein — contains 0g tryptophan and low leucine ~3g/100g), 0g fat, 0g carbohydrate. Per standard 10g serving (1 sachet or scoop): ~38 kcal, ~9g collagen protein. Key amino acids per 10g serving: glycine ~3g (most abundant), proline ~1.7g, hydroxyproline ~1.4g — the characteristic imino acid profile of collagen. Vitamin C co-supplementation is required for in vivo collagen synthesis (ascorbate is a cofactor for prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase enzymes). Clinical evidence: skin elasticity improvement supported by meta-analysis of 19 RCTs (Choi et al. 2019, Nutrients); joint health (osteoarthritis, sports-related joint pain) supported by medium-quality evidence. NOT a substitute for complete protein sources (whey, egg, meat). GI is not applicable (0g carbohydrate). Available in Singapore at Watsons, Guardian, Unity Pharmacy, Cold Storage, and Lazada as standalone powder and ready-to-drink collagen beverages. Major commercial brands include Peptan (Rousselot SAS, France), GELITA (Germany), Verisol (GELITA), and NeoCell. Dietary flags: halal-certified (bovine variant from halal-slaughtered cattle), gluten-free, dairy-free. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians.

confidence_reason: Macronutrient profile (90g protein, 0g fat, 0g carbohydrate, ~380 kcal per 100g) is consistent with USDA FoodData Central gelatin data (FDC ID 169567) and is well-supported by multiple commercial manufacturer technical data sheets (Peptan/Rousselot, GELITA AG). Amino acid profile is consistent with published collagen biochemistry. Clinical evidence citations (Choi 2019, Gribble 2016, Ohara 2010, Zhong 2003) are from peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is HIGH.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is naturally zero — hydrolysed collagen peptides are a pure protein isolate with all lipid content removed during enzymatic hydrolysis and processing; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central gelatin (FDC 169567) and commercial collagen powder specifications. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — collagen contains no carbohydrate by biochemical composition; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central gelatin (FDC 169567) and manufacturer data. Dietary fibre is naturally zero by product composition. Cholesterol is confirmed zero per USDA gelatin data. All vitamins (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, B-vitamins, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero — hydrolysed collagen peptides are a pure protein supplement with no vitamin content; vitamin C must be co-supplemented separately for in vivo collagen synthesis. Selenium is confirmed zero per USDA gelatin data. GLYCEMIC INDEX: Not applicable — this food contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g; glycemic index testing is not performed for zero-carbohydrate protein supplements. GI set to null, gi_category set to not_applicable per Project Veritas critical rule. PROTEIN COMPLETENESS NOTE: Hydrolysed collagen is NOT a complete protein — it contains 0g tryptophan and low leucine (~3g/100g); it should not be used as a sole protein source. WATER CONTENT: ~8g water per 100g dry powder (typical moisture content for spray-dried collagen powder; consistent with USDA gelatin data). MONOUNSATURATED AND POLYUNSATURATED FAT: Set to 0.0 — confirmed zero, pure protein product with no fat fraction per USDA FoodData Central gelatin (FDC 169567). TRANS FAT: Set to 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation. SOURCE ADAPTATION: USDA FoodData Central gelatin dry powder unsweetened (FDC ID 169567) used as closest approved-source equivalent; hydrolysed collagen peptides are enzymatically processed gelatin derivatives with essentially identical macronutrient profile. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 (collagen, food component) used as closest defensible parent; FoodEx2 A0CKP (Protein preparations) used as closest defensible parent category. No SNOMED CT code assigned — no confirmed code available in public browser as of 2026. HALAL NOTE: Halal status applies to bovine collagen from halal-certified sources; marine collagen is generally considered halal. Kosher status not confirmed for all commercial variants. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array including romanized entry.
